# John Klingelhofer

Senior Software Engineer specializing in AI/ML — multi-agent systems, computer vision, and data engineering for healthcare and pharma. 8 years of experience across the full stack. Based in Brooklyn, NY.

## About

I'm a Senior Software Engineer at Formation Bio, where I build AI-driven applications that accelerate drug development and clinical trials. My work spans the full stack: Python and TypeScript (React, NestJS) for web applications, computer vision for extracting structured data from scientific literature, multi-agent LLM systems for complex decision-making, and agentic data pipelines.

I'm drawn to meaningful, complex problems that improve people's lives — particularly in healthcare, medicine, and education.

Open to senior IC and tech lead roles in AI/ML. Currently on parental leave through July 2026; not immediately available, but always glad to hear from you.

## Experience

### Formation Bio — Senior Software Engineer (Aug 2023 – Present)
New York, NY

Senior IC driving AI/ML initiatives across the full stack — NLP, computer vision, predictive modeling, and data infrastructure, from research and prototyping through production. Most projects are internal and proprietary; Muse (below) is a rare public exception.

**Featured Project: Muse** (2024)
Led backend architecture and implementation for Muse, an LLM-driven suite of tools that generates human-level, regulation-compliant recruitment materials for clinical trials — the result of a partnership with Sanofi and OpenAI. Built before agentic AI frameworks were widely available, Muse was a novel multi-agent system that brought AI-generated, regulation-compliant recruitment materials into production in the highly regulated pharmaceutical industry. The project was a major company success and is now used in the real world by Sanofi.

"We believe AI can accelerate drug development, bringing new treatments to patients more quickly... we can't wait to see the impact Muse will have." — Brad Lightcap, OpenAI COO

"The development of Muse represents another proof point in Sanofi's journey to becoming the first pharma company powered by AI at scale." — Emmanuel Frenehard, Sanofi CDO

Across other initiatives: built custom scrapers and specialized extraction pipelines for pulling structured data from tables in medical literature; built Dagster-based ingestion and transformation pipelines for drug and indication datasets; developed predictive models for drug outcome forecasting and automated indication expansion.

### Formation Bio — Software Engineer II (Oct 2021 – Jul 2023)
New York, NY

Fullstack contributions to multiple applications supporting form-building and deployment/management for nurse/patient use in clinical trial operations. (NestJS, Python, React)

### Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center — Software Engineer (Jan 2021 – Oct 2021)
New York, NY

Automated, optimized, and expanded a system for transforming vast amounts of hospital data from disparate sources into the OMOP Common Data Model, facilitating use in MSK's cancer research. (Python, SQL, Airflow)

### Annalect — Software Engineer (Nov 2020 – Jan 2021)
New York, NY

Full stack developer on core applications within Omni, the Marketing Science and Data software suite used across Omnicom Media Group agencies. (Python, AngularJS, Redshift, AWS)

### Annalect — Junior Developer (Jan 2019 – Nov 2020)
New York, NY

Full stack development and ML feature implementation across Omni applications; built data ingestion pipelines and lookalike modeling for audience expansion.

### Annalect — R&D Engineering Intern (Sep 2018 – Dec 2018)
New York, NY

Prototyped experimental applications within the Annalect Labs team. Built a recommendation engine using Keras to derive correlations between brands' shoppers from clickstream data.

## Education

**Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute** — BS Computer Science, Cum Laude
Concentration in AI and Data · Class of 2018 · Troy, NY

**Clark University** — Liberal Arts (two years before transferring to RPI)
2012–2014 · Worcester, MA
